summary: Matched six particle/baselib functions in src/sysdolphin/baselib/particle.c and made a small API type correction so surrounding callers keep matching. The PR primarily used local pointer aliases, inline scheduling tweaks, deliberate redundant-looking null checks, and prototype widening to resolve register allocation, inlining, and caller-side narrowing issues. It also improved match reports for several nearby particle functions.
what_changed: Matched six functions in src/sysdolphin/baselib/particle.c: hsd_80393D2C, hsd_803966A0, hsd_80396C78, fn_80397374, hsd_8039EFAC, and hsd_8039F6CC.
Changed hsd_8039F05C's first parameter type from s8 to s32 in both particle.c and particle.h.
Changed efLib_CreateGenerator's local linkNo from u8 to int so the caller remains matched after hsd_8039F05C's prototype was widened.
Reworked repeated direct global accesses to hsd_804CF7E8 and hsd_804CF810 by introducing local struct pointer aliases such as struct ParticleConsoleState* sp and struct ParticleScreenState* sp.
Adjusted ps_remove_node's head pointer assignment into the if condition: if (*(head_ptr = &sp->xD0) == NULL), apparently to influence inline scheduling.
Added #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around hsd_80393D2C.
For hsd_8039EFAC and hsd_8039F6CC, added an extra if (gen != NULL) block after an early NULL return and guarded ref_INC(jobj) with if (jobj != NULL).
Removed several stale TODO comments for now-matched functions and updated the remaining hsd_8039F05C TODO from relocation-difference wording to a .bss.0 section-anchor/register-allocation issue.
Reported additional match improvements for hsd_80395D88, hsd_803962A8, hsd_80396A20, fn_80397814, and hsd_8039F05C.
smart_moves: Widening hsd_8039F05C(linkNo) from s8 to s32 aligned it with the surrounding particle API convention of int/s32 linkNo and removed spurious caller-side narrowing.
The caller-side efLib_CreateGenerator local was widened from u8 to int at the same time, avoiding a fix that would improve the callee while regressing the caller match.
Using local aliases for global state structs, especially hsd_804CF810, reduced repeated address expressions and helped match register allocation in particle screen-state functions.
The ps_remove_node assignment-in-condition form targeted a specific addi/lwz ordering problem called out by old TODO comments.

Keeping redundant-looking gen != NULL checks after an early return in hsd_8039EFAC and hsd_8039F6CC reproduced target control flow/dead-branch structure rather than simplifying the C away from the binary.
Guarding ref_INC(jobj) with a NULL check preserved safer semantics while also contributing to matching the generator creation functions.
The PR kept a clear distinction between fully matched functions and a still-unmatched hsd_8039F05C, updating its TODO with the more precise current blocker.
decomp_lessons: For particle APIs, linkNo should be treated as s32/int when surrounding functions use that convention; an overly narrow s8/u8 type can create unwanted caller-side extension or truncation code.
Local pointer aliases to global state structs can be necessary for matching, not just readability, because they influence register allocation and address materialization.
Do not automatically remove redundant NULL checks or apparently dead branches in decomp code; target binaries may contain those shapes and matching may require preserving them.
Inline helper scheduling can hinge on small source-level expression choices, such as assigning a pointer inside a condition instead of on the previous statement.
When fixing a callee prototype, inspect all callers for narrowing/sign-extension fallout and adjust local variable types if needed.
A TODO should identify the current concrete blocker, such as section-anchor hoisting or register-allocation cascade, rather than an outdated symptom like relocation differences.
naming_conventions: Particle generator link number parameters are named linkNo and should use s32/int in these APIs.
Local aliases for particle state structs used the short name sp for struct ParticleConsoleState* and struct ParticleScreenState*.
Existing anonymous/unmatched symbol names were preserved, including hsd_80393D2C, hsd_803966A0, hsd_80396C78, fn_80397374, hsd_8039EFAC, hsd_8039F05C, and hsd_8039F6CC.
The public declaration in particle.h mirrors the source signature style: HSD_Generator* hsd_8039F05C(s32 linkNo, s32 bank, s32 idx).
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #pragma dont_inline around hsd_80393D2C to control code generation.
Introduced local struct pointer aliases for globals hsd_804CF7E8 and hsd_804CF810 to guide register allocation.
Changed ps_remove_node from a separate head_ptr assignment plus NULL check to assignment inside the dereference condition to influence inline instruction ordering.
Preserved redundant control-flow shape in hsd_8039EFAC and hsd_8039F6CC with an extra gen != NULL check after an early return.
Added jobj != NULL guards before ref_INC(jobj), affecting branch layout in generator creation functions.
Changed hsd_8039F05C's parameter width from s8 to s32 to avoid caller-side narrowing and extension artifacts.
Updated hsd_8039F05C's remaining mismatch note to identify .bss.0 section anchor hoist causing a register-allocation cascade, extra saved register, and frame shift.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body or summary states verification was run with python configure.py --require-protos and ninja, and that the GALE01 report showed the six listed functions at 100.0%.
follow_up_queries: Find other particle generator APIs where linkNo is still typed as s8 or u8 and verify whether they should be s32/int.
Inspect remaining hsd_8039F05C mismatch for .bss.0 section-anchor hoist and register-allocation cascade.
Search for TODO comments in particle.c that mention inline scheduling, dead beq instructions, or register allocation and see if similar source-shaping tactics apply.
Check other callers of hsd_8039F05C for local linkNo narrowing after the prototype change.
